perturbation.original_text: The Recipient undertakes not to use the Confidential Information disclosed by the other party for any purpose except the Purpose, without first obtaining the written agreement of the other party.
perturbation.changed_text: The User undertakes not to exploit the Confidential Information disclosed by the other party for any reason except the Purpose, without first obtaining the written agreement of the other party.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Recipient' to 'User' and 'use' to 'exploit' introduces an entirely different connotation. 'Exploit' suggests a more aggressive or commercial application of the information. This introduces in-text contradiction in relation to other clauses that assumes use. Particularly in clause 7 which implies the return of the information
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Recipient will, on request from the other party, return all copies and records of the Confidential Information disclosed by the other party to the Recipient and will not retain any copies or records of the Confidential Information disclosed by the other party.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Recipient' to 'User' and 'use' to 'exploit' introduces a contradiction. The original text discusses the 'Recipient' and their obligation to return 'copies and records of the Confidential Information'. The changed text replaces 'Recipient' with 'User' and 'use' with 'exploit'. 'Exploit' has a different connotation than 'use', implying a more aggressive commercial application. This contradicts the original intent of the contradicted text, which implies the simple return of the 'Confidential Information' as was initially disclosed. This aligns with the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' perturbation definition as it uses 'User' and 'exploit' without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations with the obligation to return copies and records. This change significantly alters the scope and nature of the user's obligations, creating a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The Recipient undertakes to keep the Confidential Information disclosed by the other party secure and not to disclose it to any third party [except to its employees [and professional advisers] who need to know the same for the Purpose, who know they owe a duty of confidence to the other party and who are bound by obligations equivalent to those in clause 3 above and this clause 4.
perturbation.changed_text: The User will endeavor to keep the Confidential Information disclosed by the other party secure and will only disclose it to its direct reports who have a need to know.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Recipient undertakes' to 'User will endeavor' significantly weakens the obligation, and the changed text creates in-text contradiction because the initial text implies a weaker commitment compared to the stricter requirement for confidentiality outlined elsewhere in the document. The original clause created a strict obligation to not disclose information to any third party, but this has been changed to direct reports. The contradiction lies in the standard of care. This contradiction impacts clause 5.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The undertakings in clauses 3 and 4 above apply to all of the information disclosed by each of the parties to the other, regardless of the way or form in which it is disclosed or recorded but they do not apply to: a) any information which is or in future comes into the public domain (unless as a result of the breach of this Agreement); or b) any information which is already known to the Recipient and which was not subject to any obligation of confidence before it was dis closed to the Recipient by the other party.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Recipient undertakes' to 'User will endeavor' weakens the obligation regarding confidential information. 'Undertakes' implies a strict requirement, while 'will endeavor' suggests a weaker, effort-based commitment. This contradicts the contradicted text, which states that 'The undertakings in clauses 3 and 4 above apply to all of the information disclosed', indicating a strong and comprehensive obligation. The weaker obligation in the changed text now clashes with the expectation of strict undertakings defined by clauses 3 and 4, creating an in-text contradiction. Furthermore, limiting disclosure to 'direct reports' contradicts the original text, which allows disclosure to 'employees and professional advisors', creating a narrower scope and conflicting with the original intent to allow disclosure to more than just 'direct reports'.

perturbation.original_text: The Recipient will, on request from the other party, return all copies and records of the Confidential Information disclosed by the other party to the Recipient and will not retain any copies or records of the Confidential Information disclosed by the other party.
perturbation.changed_text: Upon written notice, the User will endeavor to delete all digital copies of the Confidential Information disclosed by the other party, but may retain physical records at their discretion.
perturbation.explanation: The change from 'return all copies and records' to 'endeavor to delete all digital copies, but may retain physical records at their discretion' creates a significant contradiction. The original clause mandated the return of all information, while the modified clause allows the retention of physical records, undermining the confidentiality agreement's purpose. The retention of physical records contradicts clause 9.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The undertakings in clauses 3 and 4 will continue in force [indefinitely][for [insert number] years from the date of this Agreement].
perturbation.justification: The original text required the return of *all* copies and records. The changed text allows the user to retain physical records, which directly contradicts the continued enforcement of the original undertaking, particularly in clauses 3 and 4, as described in the Perturbation Explanation. The fact that the confidentiality clauses will continue to be in effect indefinitely or for a certain number of years is incompatible with retaining physical records.

perturbation.original_text: Neither this Agreement nor the supply of any information grants the Recipient any licence, interest or right in respect of any intellectual property rights of the other party except the right to copy the Confidential Information disclosed by the other party solely for the Purpose.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement grants the User a limited license to utilize the Confidential Information for internal business development, subject to prior written consent from the disclosing party.
perturbation.explanation: The original text strictly prohibits granting any license or right to the Recipient except for copying for the stated Purpose. The modified text directly contradicts this by granting a limited license to 'utilize' the Confidential Information for 'internal business development'. This introduces in-text contradiction, especially when compared to the initial limitation of use stated in clause 3.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Recipient undertakes not to use the Confidential Information disclosed by the other party for any purpose except the Purpose, without first obtaining the written agreement of the other party.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly prohibits granting any license except for copying, whereas the changed text grants a limited license for 'internal business development.' This directly contradicts the 'Contradicted Text' that states the Recipient cannot use the information for any purpose except the Purpose without written agreement. This contradiction is clear and impactful, as it creates uncertainty about the scope of permitted use.
